assertions.description: The @function at-rule is recognized by the CSS parser.
A dashed custom function can be called inside a property value.
A custom function with typed parameters and returns <color> serializes through CSSOM.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@function --tone(--base <color>, --amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--base), white var(--amount)); }');return s.cssRules.length===1&&s.cssRules[0].cssText.includes('returns <color>');}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: A valid custom function call computes to a non-transparent color.
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --tone(--base <color>, --amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--base), white var(--amount)); }.p{background:--tone(red,20%)}';const p=document.createElement('div');p.className='p';document.body.append(st,p);const bg=getComputedStyle(p).backgroundColor;st.remove();p.remove();return bg!=='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'&&bg!=='';})()

assertions.description: Omitting an argument uses the function parameter's default value.
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --tone(--base <color>, --amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--base), white var(--amount)); }.a{background:--tone(red,20%)}.b{background:--tone(red)}';const a=document.createElement('div');const b=document.createElement('div');a.className='a';b.className='b';document.body.append(st,a,b);const ok=getComputedStyle(a).backgroundColor===getComputedStyle(b).backgroundColor&&getComputedStyle(a).backgroundColor!=='';st.remove();a.remove();b.remove();return ok;})()

assertions.description: Passing a <length> to a <color> parameter rejects the custom function result.
A function body can read a custom property from the call site with var().
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --brand-tone(--amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { --local-brand: var(--brand); result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--local-brand), white var(--amount)); }.p{--brand:red;background:--brand-tone(20%)}';const p=document.createElement('div');p.className='p';document.body.append(st,p);const bg=getComputedStyle(p).backgroundColor;st.remove();p.remove();return bg!=='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'&&bg!=='';})()

assertions.description: A custom function can call another custom function and return its computed value.
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --tone(--base <color>, --amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--base), white var(--amount)); }@function --brand-tone(--base <color>) returns <color> { result: --tone(var(--base), 20%); }.p{background:--brand-tone(red)}';const p=document.createElement('div');p.className='p';document.body.append(st,p);const bg=getComputedStyle(p).backgroundColor;st.remove();p.remove();return bg!=='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'&&bg!=='';})()

assertions.description: A 0% percentage argument remains a valid boundary value.
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --tone(--base <color>, --amount <percentage>: 20%) returns <color> { result: color-mix(in oklch, var(--base), white var(--amount)); }.p{background:--tone(red,0%)}';const p=document.createElement('div');p.className='p';document.body.append(st,p);const bg=getComputedStyle(p).backgroundColor;st.remove();p.remove();return bg!=='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'&&bg!=='';})()

assertions.description: A <number> parameter can drive an alpha channel in the function result.
assertions.test: (()=>{const st=document.createElement('style');st.textContent='@function --alpha-tone(--base <color>, --opacity <number>: .5) returns <color> { result: oklch(from var(--base) l c h / var(--opacity)); }.p{background:--alpha-tone(red,.5)}';const p=document.createElement('div');p.className='p';document.body.append(st,p);const bg=getComputedStyle(p).backgroundColor;st.remove();p.remove();return bg!=='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0)'&&bg!=='';})()
